One Link
How it works Features Security Share Get One Link
Verify your download

Hash the binary in your browser.

Drop your downloaded One Link file into the box. Your browser computes the SHA-256 locally with Web Crypto. The file never leaves your device. We then fetch the live signed value from the release relay and compare. Match means the binary is byte-for-byte what we shipped.

What this does: reads your file's bytes locally, runs SHA-256 over them via crypto.subtle.digest (Web Crypto), then fetches the live signed hash from HEAD /download/<os> on the release relay. Both happen in your tab; no upload, no server-side processing. The match check is byte-equal.

Why this matters: Windows SmartScreen and macOS Gatekeeper warn you because the binary is new, not because anything is wrong with it. This page proves the bytes you have match the bytes the maintainer signed. If the hashes match, the file is exactly what we shipped, even if your OS has not learned to trust it yet.

If the hashes do not match: something between you and us altered the file. Do not run it. Try the download again, or download the source archive and build it yourself.

For the full cryptographic receipt (Ed25519 signature, build environment attestation, source commit) see /security/ and the attestation chain endpoint at /api/attest/<sha>.

One Link

A free, private network for messages, files, and devices. Owned by no one. Belongs to everyone.
